message:
refactor(core): migrate remaining call sites to scope-free read_inode, read_group_desc, and lookup_name

The previous commit (c6915f4) introduced scope-free convenience methods
that omit the `&RequestScope::empty()` parameter for internal operations
that don't need request-scoped tracking. This commit completes the
migration by updating all remaining call sites throughout lib.rs:

- fsck orphan recovery (read_inode in orphan list traversal)
- journal recovery (read_inode for journal inode lookup)
- group stats collection (read_group_desc iteration)
- deleted inode list traversal (read_inode for dtime chain)
- bitmap reading (read_group_desc for block/inode bitmaps)
- consistency verification (read_group_desc for free counts)
- directory operations: mkdir, mknod, unlink/rmdir, link, rename,
  symlink, truncate, fallocate, setattr, setxattr, removexattr
  (read_inode, lookup_name, read_group_desc throughout)
- FsOps trait implementations: lookup, readdir, read, readlink,
  listxattr, getxattr (read_inode calls)

This is a pure mechanical refactor — no behavioral changes. Every call
that previously passed `&RequestScope::empty()` now uses the shorter
form, improving readability and consistency across ~45 call sites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
